title: Omkareshwar Temple
tagline: Omkareshwar Temple — spiritual town of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Omkareshwar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva, located in Mandhata, nearby Khandwa city in Khandwa district of the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.
overview.description: Omkareshwar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva, located in Mandhata, nearby Khandwa city in Khandwa district of the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. It is one of the 12 revered Jyotirlinga shrines of Shiva. It is on an island called Mandhata, near Khandwa city in the Narmada River at Khandwa district in Madhya Pradesh, India; the shape of the island is said to be like the Devanagari ॐ symbol. There are two main temples of Shiva here, one to Omkareshwar (whose name means "Lord of Omkara or the Lord of the Om sound") located in the island and one to Mamleshwar (Amaleshwar) (whose name me

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Mandhata
topPlaces.description: Mandhata, also called Omkareshwar or Shivapuri and ancient Mahishmati, capital of Avanti Mahajanpada is a riverine island in the Narmada River in Khandwa district, Madhya Pradesh, India. Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga is situated on the southern part of the island.

topPlaces.name: Omkareshwar Dam
topPlaces.description: The Omkareshwar Dam is a gravity dam on the Narmada River just upstream of Mandhata in Khandwa district, Madhya Pradesh, India. It is named after the Omkareshwar temple located just downstream.

topPlaces.name: Kaveri River (Madhya Pradesh)
topPlaces.description: The Kaveri is a tributary of the Narmada River in Madhya Pradesh, India. It has a length of 40 km, and a catchment area of 954 km2.

topPlaces.name: Omkareshwar Floating Solar Power Park
topPlaces.description: Omkareshwar Floating Solar Power Park is a floating photovoltaic power station at Omkareshwar Dam, Khandwa, India.

topPlaces.name: Choral River
topPlaces.description: The Choral River is a tributary of the Narmada River of Central India.

items.note: Mandhata, also called Omkareshwar or Shivapuri and ancient Mahishmati, capital of Avanti Mahajanpada is a riverine island in the Narmada River in Khandwa district, Madhya Pradesh, India. Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga is situated on the southern part of the island.
The Omkareshwar Dam is a gravity dam on the Narmada River just upstream of Mandhata in Khandwa district, Madhya Pradesh, India. It is named after the Omkareshwar temple located just downstream.
The Kaveri is a tributary of the Narmada River in Madhya Pradesh, India. It has a length of 40 km, and a catchment area of 954 km2.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Omkareshwar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Omkareshwar Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Omkareshwar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Devi Ahilya Bai Holkar Airport, Indore (64 km) and the nearest railway station is Itarsi Junction (171 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Omkareshwar Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Omkareshwar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Omkareshwar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Omkareshwar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Omkareshwar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mandhata, Omkareshwar Dam, Kaveri River (Madhya Pradesh), Omkareshwar Floating Solar Power Park, Choral River.
